codeReview.summary: Re-reviewed commit 25e83bb7c5d1dec27b2de2484f6ce5ed9a74fc1a, the worker handoff, transcript skeleton, prior scrutiny report, and the current createReviewTask contract. The rerun still changes only the test name in `src/composables/useUserStore.createReviewTask.test.ts`, and that clarified expectation remains accurate because `useUserStore.createReviewTask()` and `ReviewTaskCreateRequest` still intentionally set `reviewerId` from `checkerId` for legacy checker/reviewer handoff compatibility. I found no blocking or non-blocking issues in scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The designer-tracking-worker skill still mandates manual agent-browser verification for all features, but this rerun confirms there are valid baseline-only repair tasks whose only change is an assertion/name update in an existing unit test. The worker skipped browser validation and that deviation is justified by the actual scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 25e83bb changes only `src/composables/useUserStore.createReviewTask.test.ts` (one renamed test title); transcript skeleton for workerSessionId `419e83eb-fb46-433d-9a2f-f62710f199f6` shows no runtime/UI file edits; the skill's Step 6 still requires agent-browser unconditionally.

summary: Pass. The current branch state still supports the prior conclusion: this fix only realigns the baseline test wording with the shipped createReviewTask contract, and the rerun introduces no review findings.
